meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Stalling
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS builds. Use this intermediate checklist featuring Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients every single time.
content.answer_block: To stop stalling, stop building from scratch. Use a pre-configured SaaS starter kit to handle authentication, database schemas, and payments. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you reduce boilerplate by 80%, allowing you to focus purely on high-value business logic and client-specific features.
content.intro:
The graveyard of unfinished SaaS projects is paved with good intentions and endless 'boilerplate hell.' You start with high energy, only to find yourself buried in the weeds of authentication flows, database migrations, and Stripe integration for the tenth time this year. The dream of a scalable product slowly suffocates under the weight of repetitive setup.

But the game has changed. You are not a code monkey; you are an architect. By adopting an industrial-grade SaaS starter kit and automating the heavy lifting with AI-driven workflows, you strip away the friction. This guide turns your development process into an assembly line, ensuring you ship before your client’s interest wanes.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Stack
steps.description: Define your schema and base architecture immediately to prevent scope creep. Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS architecture using Next.js 14, Supabase for auth/DB, and Tailwind CSS. Include a folder structure for modular feature development.

steps.title: Automating Auth and User Flow
steps.description: Authentication is a time-sink. Use pre-built modules to implement secure sign-in, sign-up, and session management in minutes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Supabase authentication flows including email magic link and Google OAuth within the existing Next.js app structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate supabase-auth middleware and protect routes'

steps.title: Stripe Integration and Billing
steps.description: Don't write billing logic from scratch. Integrate a ready-made Stripe checkout flow that handles webhooks and subscription states automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription service module that handles webhook events for payment success and subscription cancellations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup stripe webhook handler and subscription database schema'

steps.title: Feature-Specific Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate CRUD operations for your specific business logic, maintaining a consistent pattern across your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable server-action pattern for CRUD operations on a 'Project' entity, including optimistic UI updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate server actions for project entity with zod validation'

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automate your deployment so you can ship a production-ready build to your client with one command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel-compatible CI/CD configuration that runs linting, tests, and deployment automatically on main branch pushes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure vercel.json and github actions workflow for deployment'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'feature-first' folder structure; it isolates bugs and allows you to swap out modules without breaking the core.
Use Zod schemas to define your data models before writing a single line of database code; it forces you to think about edge cases early.
Never manually write boilerplate; if you find yourself typing the same pattern twice, build a script or prompt to automate it permanently.
Force yourself to deploy to a staging URL on Day 1; the psychological win of seeing your app live keeps your momentum high.
Keep your Claude Code context window lean; use .claudeignore to exclude massive node_modules or build artifacts to keep AI responses accurate.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, begin a feedback loop with your client. Use the insights gained to iterate on the features, not the architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on setup and start delivering value to your clients today. Our proven framework is the shortcut you've been looking for. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
